figures.caption: Table 1. Quantitative performance of VIVID-Med and baselines on in-domain (CheXpert) and cross-domain (NIH) tasks. Results are reported as mean±std over 3 seeds. NIH results are evaluated purely zero-shot without retraining.
Table 2. Cross-modality transfer: CXR-pretrained ViT → CT slice classification (mean±std, 3 seeds). No CT data is used during pretraining.

Fig. 1. VIVID-Med overview. The frozen LLM provides structured token supervision during training only. Gradients update the parameters of the ViT and SPD projector, while the deployed model retains only the lightweight ViT backbone.
Table 3. Ablation studies isolating the impact of supervision and architecture on the CheXpert linear probe task. Results are reported as mean±std over 3 seeds.

Fig. 2. Structured Prediction Decomposition (SPD). Multiple query groups perform cross-attention over shared ViT tokens. Orthogonality regularization encourages complementary branches before shared projection to the LLM embedding space.
Fig. 3. SPD cross-attention maps for a frontal (top) and lateral (bottom) CXR. Each group attends to distinct anatomical regions.
